title: resolve_path is 60-line function with cyclomatic complexity > 15
description:
## Severity
Major

## Dimension
Complexity / Cognitive Readability

## Location
src/tools/file/mod.rs:119-185 (`resolve_path` function)

## Violation
This function combines: relative-to-absolute join with env lookup, `..` rejection, canonicalize-or-walk-up-ancestors with an unbounded `loop` (lines 145-158) and a manually built suffix vector, strict-mode env-var check, dual-jail (PROJECT_ROOT OR TEMP_ROOT) check, and error formatting. Each step has multiple branches → cyclomatic ≈ 18. The middle ancestor-walk is a mutable, fallible loop building a Vec inside a let-binding. Cognitive complexity is over the §3.5 threshold.

## Mandated refactor
Extract three private free-functions:
1. `fn absolutize(p: &str) -> Result<PathBuf, String>` for steps 1+2.
2. `fn canonicalize_or_walk_up(absolute: &Path) -> Result<PathBuf, String>` for the loop (testable in isolation against synthetic non-existent paths).
3. `fn enforce_jail(canonical: &Path) -> Result<(), String>` for the dual-jail check.

`resolve_path` becomes a linear three-step function under 15 lines.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
